verifyNote: Ideas and general info only — not legal, tax, or business advice. A valid H-4 EAD lets you work for any employer or be self-employed (W-2, 1099, freelance, sole proprietor, LLC, part-time) WHILE the EAD is valid. Verify your specifics with an immigration attorney and a CPA.

pillars.label: Hyper-local & micro-businesses
pillars.blurb: Low-overhead businesses you can run from home or your neighborhood.

cards.title: In-home daycare
cards.idealFor: People who love kids and want flexible, home-based income.
cards.setupTax: Usually a sole proprietorship to start; many register an LLC for liability. Income is self-employment (Schedule C) — set aside for self-employment tax and quarterly estimates.
cards.caveat: Child-care licensing and the number of children allowed VARY BY STATE and change often. Use the state checker below and verify with your state's official licensing authority BEFORE accepting any children.

cards.title: Indian tiffin / meal-prep
cards.idealFor: Great home cooks serving the local desi community.
cards.setupTax: Sole prop or LLC; track ingredient costs and 1099/cash income on Schedule C.
cards.caveat: Selling food from home is regulated. Most states require a cottage-food registration and/or a health permit, and some prohibit certain foods. Verify your state and county health-department rules before selling.

cards.title: Festive & boutique apparel rental
cards.idealFor: Style-savvy spouses with an eye for ethnic wear.
cards.setupTax: Sole prop to start; report income on Schedule C. Collect/remit sales tax where required.
cards.caveat: Sales-tax registration may be required in your state. General info — confirm with a CPA.

cards.title: Mehndi & event services
cards.idealFor: Artists and organizers for weddings and festivals.
cards.setupTax: Typically sole prop; 1099 / cash income on Schedule C.
cards.caveat: Some services need local business or cosmetology permits. Verify locally.

cards.title: Tutoring local kids
cards.setupTax: Sole prop; Schedule C self-employment income.
cards.caveat: General info — no special license usually needed, but verify local rules.

pillars.label: Digital & remote freelancing
pillars.blurb: Work from anywhere, often as a 1099 contractor or your own LLC.

cards.title: Online tutoring
cards.idealFor: Strong in math, coding, or languages.
cards.setupTax: Platform 1099 or your own invoicing; Schedule C, self-employment tax.
cards.caveat: General info only — confirm tax treatment with a CPA.

cards.title: Virtual assistance
cards.setupTax: 1099 contractor; Schedule C. Consider an LLC as you grow.

cards.title: Social-media management
cards.caveat: General info — confirm with a CPA.

cards.title: Bookkeeping
cards.caveat: A bookkeeping certificate helps credibility. General info only.

pillars.label: Upskill & launch
pillars.blurb: Build skills now, then work or intern — no need to switch to F-1 with a valid EAD.

cards.title: Community-college certificates
cards.idealFor: Career switchers wanting a credential fast.
cards.setupTax: Employment after is usually W-2; a valid EAD lets you work without switching to F-1.
cards.caveat: Confirm program details and any work components with the school and an attorney.

cards.title: Tech bootcamps
cards.setupTax: Roles after are often W-2; with a valid EAD you can intern or work without F-1.
cards.caveat: General info — verify any internship/work terms with an attorney.

cards.title: Intern or work while studying
cards.setupTax: W-2 or 1099 depending on the role.
cards.caveat: A valid H-4 EAD authorizes this; you do NOT need to change to F-1. Verify your EAD validity dates first.
